story.iWant: a comprehensive performance benchmarking framework that measures conversion speed, memory usage, and WASM binary size
story.soThat: I can validate Recipe meets its <100ms WASM conversion target, detect performance regressions, and maintain optimal user experience across all interfaces
story.tasks: - Implement benchmark suite with 6 conversion path benchmarks (AC-1) - Validate performance targets: WASM <100ms, CLI <20ms (AC-2) - Measure WASM binary size: unstripped, stripped, gzipped (AC-3) - Create automation scripts: benchmark.sh and benchmark.ps1 (AC-4) - Document baseline performance in docs/performance-benchmarks.md (AC-5) - Integrate benchmarks into CI/CD with GitHub Actions (AC-6) - Document profiling tools and workflow (AC-7) - Compare Go v2.0 to Python v1.0 baseline (AC-8) - Update README with performance claims and documentation links (Task 9)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: Go Benchmark Suite Implementation Benchmark functions exist for all 6 conversion paths (NP3↔XMP, XMP↔LRT, NP3↔LRT). Benchmarks use representative sample files from testdata/ or examples/. Benchmarks report: ns/op, B/op, allocs/op. Benchmarks run successfully via: go test -bench=. ./internal/converter/
### AC-2: Performance Targets Validation WASM Conversion: <100ms (100,000,000 ns/op) PRIMARY TARGET. CLI Conversion: <20ms (20,000,000 ns/op) STRETCH GOAL. Batch Processing: <2s for 100 files (already proven: 37ms in Story 3-3). Memory Allocation: <4096 B/op per conversion. Memory Allocations: <12 allocs/op per conversion. Results documented in docs/performance-benchmarks.md.
### AC-3: WASM Binary Size Validation Unstripped binary: <5MB. Stripped binary: <2MB (production target). Gzipped binary: <800KB (CDN delivery target). Size measurements automated in build process. Binary size regression detection (<10% growth allowed).
### AC-4: Benchmark Automation Script Script exists at scripts/benchmark.sh (Unix) and scripts/benchmark.ps1 (Windows). Script runs all benchmarks with consistent configuration. Script outputs results to benchmarks.txt. Script validates results against targets (exit code 1 if targets missed). Script usage documented in README.
### AC-5: Baseline Performance Documentation Baseline results documented in docs/performance-benchmarks.md. Includes benchmark output for all conversion paths. Documents test environment (CPU, RAM, Go version, OS). Establishes regression thresholds (>10% slowdown = regression). Updated with each major release.
### AC-6: CI/CD Integration Benchmark job exists in .github/workflows/test.yml or benchmark.yml. Benchmarks run on every push to main branch. Benchmark results saved as CI artifacts. Performance regression detection: PR fails if >10% slower than baseline. Manual override available with justification.
### AC-7: Performance Profiling Tools CPU profiling command documented. Memory profiling command documented. Profile visualization documented. Profiling workflow documented in docs/performance-benchmarks.md. Example profiles generated and analyzed.
### AC-8: Comparison to Python v1.0 Baseline Python v1.0 baseline documented (if available). Go v2.0 speedup calculated (10-100x target). Comparison table included in docs/performance-benchmarks.md. Validates migration to Go was justified.

docs.doc: Performance targets: <2 second conversion time in browser. CLI batch processing 53x faster than target (37ms vs 2s for 100 files, proven in Story 3-3). Web interface feels instant.
Performance targets: <100ms WASM conversions, 10-100x faster than Python v1.0. Single conversion API shared across all interfaces. Zero dependencies: Core library uses only Go standard library.

Go benchmarks validate <100ms WASM target, <20ms CLI target, <2s batch (100 files). Memory profiling ensures <50MB WASM heap, <100MB CLI batch. WASM binary size <2MB stripped, <800KB gzipped. Measurement via go test -benchmem reports B/op and allocs/op.
Performance: 37ms for 100 files (53x faster than 2s target). Exceptional implementation. Production ready.

file.reason: Existing benchmark suite for all 6 conversion paths. Foundation for AC-1 and AC-2. Uses examples/ directory for sample files.
Core conversion API being benchmarked. Performance-critical code path. Auto-detection overhead measured separately.
Existing build targets. Need to add optimized WASM build with -ldflags for AC-3.
Sample NP3 files used by benchmarks for NP3 conversion paths.
Sample XMP files used by benchmarks for XMP conversion paths.
Sample lrtemplate files used by benchmarks for LRT conversion paths.
Main documentation. Need to add benchmarking section with performance claims (AC-8, Task 9).

package.name: runtime/pprof
package.usage: CPU and memory profiling for AC-7

package.name: github.com/spf13/cobra
package.usage: CLI framework (CLI being benchmarked uses it)

story-context.constraints:
### Development Constraints
Zero External Dependencies: Benchmarks use Go stdlib only (testing package). No third-party frameworks. Profiling uses built-in runtime/pprof.
Performance Targets (Non-Negotiable): WASM <100ms (PRIMARY), CLI <20ms (STRETCH), Batch <2s for 100 files (achieved: 37ms). Memory: <4096 B/op, <12 allocs/op per conversion. WASM Binary Size: Unstripped <5MB, Stripped <2MB (production), Gzipped <800KB (CDN delivery).
Regression Detection: Performance degradation >10% fails CI. Memory growth >20% requires review. Binary size growth >10% blocks deployment.
### Testing Requirements
Benchmark Coverage: All 6 bidirectional conversion paths. Auto-detection overhead measured separately. Hub-and-spoke overhead isolated.
Sample Files: Use representative files from examples/ or testdata/. Cover typical use cases. Same files for consistency.
Profiling Workflow: CPU profiling for bottlenecks. Memory profiling for allocations. Visualization via go tool pprof web UI.
### Documentation Requirements
Performance Documentation: Baseline in docs/performance-benchmarks.md. Test environment details. Regression thresholds. Historical tracking.
User Documentation: Performance claims in README backed by benchmarks. Link to detailed docs. Migration justification (10-100x speedup).
### CI/CD Requirements
Automation: Benchmarks on every push to main. Results saved as CI artifacts. WASM binary size validated.
Quality Gates: PR blocked if >10% slower. PR blocked if WASM >2MB. Manual override with justification.
story-context.interfaces:
### Go Testing API
Benchmark execution: go test -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/converter/ Output format: BenchmarkName-N iterations ns/op B/op allocs/op Example: BenchmarkConvert_NP3_to_XMP-8 100000 10234 ns/op 2048 B/op 8 allocs/op
Benchmark functions use b.ResetTimer() before loop, b.Fatalf() for errors, b.Skip() for conditional execution.
### Converter API
Convert function: Convert(input []byte, fromFormat, toFormat string) ([]byte, error) - input: Source preset data - fromFormat: Source format or empty for auto-detect - toFormat: Target format - Returns: Converted data or error
DetectFormat function: DetectFormat(data []byte) (string, error) - Auto-detects format from content - Used when fromFormat is empty
### Profiling API
CPU Profiling: go test -bench=X -cpuprofile=cpu.prof ./internal/converter/ Memory Profiling: go test -bench=X -memprofile=mem.prof ./internal/converter/ Visualization: go tool pprof -http=:8080 cpu.prof
Profile Analysis: Flame graphs (CPU by function), Call graphs (execution flow), Top functions (time/memory), Line-level profiling.
### CI/CD Integration
Workflow triggers on push to main and pull requests. Jobs: checkout, setup Go 1.24, run benchmarks, upload artifacts. Results saved to benchmarks.txt for historical comparison.
### Makefile Build
WASM target (to add): GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go Benchmark target (to add): go test -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/converter/ | tee benchmarks.txt ### Automation Scripts
benchmark.sh (Unix): Runs benchmarks, validates with grep, exits 1 on failure. benchmark.ps1 (Windows): Runs benchmarks with Tee-Object, validates with Select-String, color output.

tests.standards:
Go Benchmark Framework (stdlib testing): Benchmark functions follow func Benchmark*(b *testing.B) naming. Use b.ResetTimer() to exclude setup. Use b.Fatalf() for fatal errors. Use b.Skip() for conditional tests. Report: ns/op, B/op, allocs/op.
Execution: Run all (go test -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/converter/), specific (go test -bench=BenchmarkX), save (pipe to file), profile (with -cpuprofile or -memprofile flags). Performance Targets: WASM <100ms, CLI <20ms, Memory <4096 B/op, Allocations <12 allocs/op.
Sample Strategy: Representative files from examples/, same files for consistency, valid presets only, glob patterns for discovery.
Profiling: CPU for bottlenecks, memory for allocations, visualization via go tool pprof -http, document workflow.
CI/CD: Automated on push to main, results as artifacts, regression detection (fail if >10% slower), WASM size validation (<2MB).
tests.locations: - internal/converter/bench_test.go (existing benchmark suite) - scripts/benchmark.sh (Unix automation, to create) - scripts/benchmark.ps1 (Windows automation, to create) - .github/workflows/benchmark.yml (CI/CD job, to create) - docs/performance-benchmarks.md (baseline documentation, to create) - benchmarks.txt (generated results, gitignored) - *.prof (profiling output, gitignored)
tests.ideas:
**AC-1 Testing:** Verify all 6 benchmarks exist. Run: go test -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/converter/. Verify output includes ns/op, B/op, allocs/op. No errors or skips. **AC-2 Testing:** Parse benchmarks.txt results. Extract ns/op values. Convert to milliseconds. Verify all <100ms (WASM) and <20ms (CLI). Verify memory: B/op <4096, allocs/op <12. Document actual vs target.
**AC-3 Testing:** Build unstripped WASM, measure size (<5MB). Build stripped with -ldflags="-s -w", measure (<2MB). Gzip and measure (<800KB). Add Makefile target. Document sizes.
**AC-4 Testing:** Create both scripts. Test execution. Verify benchmarks.txt created. Verify exit codes. Test on respective platforms. Document in README.
**AC-5 Testing:** Create docs/performance-benchmarks.md. Document environment, results table (all 6 paths), WASM sizes, batch results (Story 3-3), regression thresholds, historical baseline.
**AC-6 Testing:** Create .github/workflows/benchmark.yml. Add jobs. Test via PR. Verify benchmarks run in CI. Verify artifacts uploaded. Verify WASM size check. Document regression approach.
**AC-7 Testing:** Add profiling section to docs. Document commands (CPU, memory, visualization). Explain workflow. Generate example profiles. Optional screenshots.
**AC-8 Testing:** Search legacy/ for Python data. If found: calculate speedup, create table. If not: note "No baseline". Add comparison section. Verify 10-100x goal.
**Integration Testing:** Full workflow (run → save → validate → pass/fail). WASM build → measure → compare. Scripts → output → exit codes. CI → jobs → artifacts. Profiling → generate → visualize.
**Regression Testing:** Save baseline. Simulate degradation. Re-run benchmarks. Verify detection. Verify CI blocks merge.
